posts.author: dirtysanchez69
posts.title: BLM released this, but I don't think they understood what it's saying.

posts.raw_content:
This is one of the clearest case studies in modern political manipulation. Let’s break it down with facts so you can spot the tactics next time.

**The Raw Facts (Not the Soundbite)**

In 2005, Donald Trump had a private, hot-mic conversation on an Access Hollywood bus with Billy Bush. He was speaking crudely as a celebrity, not a politician:

“I just start kissing them. It’s like a magnet. Just kiss. I don’t even wait. And when you’re a star, they let you do it. You can do anything. Grab them by the pussy. You can do anything.”

Trump later called it “locker room talk” — boastful, vulgar male bragging that doesn’t reflect literal actions. He apologized for offending people. This was 11 years before he ran for president.

**The Manipulation Playbook (October 7, 2016)**

The Washington Post released the tape just weeks before Election Day — classic October surprise timing.

**Key tactics used:**

**Decontextualization**
The full boastful, trash-talking conversation was reduced to one isolated crude line. Media looped the soundbite endlessly without the “when you’re a star, they let you do it” celebrity context or the rest of the dialogue.

**False Framing**
It was sold as an “admission of sexual assault” rather than private vulgarity. Trump was portrayed as uniquely evil for words spoken off-camera, while similar (or worse) behavior from others was memory-holed.

**Selective Outrage & Hypocrisy**
Bill Clinton faced credible accusations of actual sexual misconduct and assault from multiple women (Juanita Broaddrick, Paula Jones, etc.) plus the Lewinsky scandal. The same networks and operatives largely defended or dismissed them. Hollywood elites with documented #MeToo records continued to lecture America. The double standard was glaring.

**Emotional Amplification**
Endless cable news loops, celebrity meltdowns, “pussy hats,” and Women’s March framing turned it into a cultural weapon. Repetition created availability bias: millions remembered the clip, not the facts.

**Intent: Character Assassination & Election Interference**
The goal wasn’t nuanced discussion of Trump’s past. It was to paint him as a monstrous threat to women and decency, making it socially toxic to support him. This fit the broader “basket of deplorables” / Russia collusion / existential danger narrative designed to shame and demoralize voters.

**The Results**

Temporary polling dip, especially with some suburban women.

Massive polarization and eroded trust.

Millions internalized the demonization without ever hearing the full story.

Trump still won in 2016 — proof that many Americans saw through the hype and prioritized policy over 11-year-old private talk.

**The Teachable Lesson**

Modern media and political operatives often don’t lie with outright falsehoods. They curate, omit, repeat, and emotionally frame partial truths to manufacture outrage and control narratives.

**How to protect yourself:**

Always demand full context and transcript.

Ask: “What’s the timing? What’s being ignored? Who benefits?”

Compare treatment of similar cases across parties.
posts.author: HeNotMedicated
posts.title: The "Grab Them by the Pussy" Smear: How a 2005 Tape Was Weaponized to Brainwash Millions

Intent: Character Assassination & Election Interference The goal wasn’t nuanced discussion of Trump’s past.
